publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-06-08 00:13:39 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Manuscripts, notes, drafts and final copies of articles; personal and professional correspondence, newspaper clippings, legislative materials, press releases, diaries, minute books, local history scrapbooks, and ephemera of William T. Doty (1847-1936), American newspaper editor, journalist, agriculturalist, New York State politician, and labor leader.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: The William T. Doty papers (1842-1945, bulk 1864-1934) document William T. Doty's career as a newspaper editor, journalist, agriculturist, historian, and politician. It contains manuscripts, notes and drafts, newspaper clippings, press releases, diaries, pamphlets, and scrapbooks. The collection primarily consists of materials related to Doty's career as a newspaperman, historian, and politician: materials include drafts of articles, manuscripts detailing the histories of journalism and printing in Orange County, New York, and correspondence from his term as New York Assemblyman, primarily related to the Foley bill and the Jackson-Murtagh labor bill. The collection also includes materials related to Doty's interest in agriculture and farming, correspondence includes letters between Doty and the United States Department of Agriculture concerning the identification of apples in New York State and ephemera materials include Farmer's Almanacs and New York State Agriculture Experimentation Station bulletins and publications. Scrapbooks and clippings in the collection cover topics such as agriculture and farming, banking laws, game laws, trust companies, crimes and scandals, philosophy and religion, politics, spiritualism, and local issues.

The William T. Doty papers includes a small amount of correspondence between members of the Brotherhood of Locomotive Fireman and Enginemen but materials relating to the founding and organization of the Brotherhood of Locomotive Fireman and Enginemen cannot be found in this collection.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: William T. Doty (1847-1936) was an American newspaper editor, journalist, agriculturalist, historian, and politician born in Sussex County, New Jersey. Over the course of his career, he worked in various capacities on newspapers in New York and New Jersey, including the Tri-State Union, the Middletown Mercury, the Banner of Liberty, the Whig Press, Sign of the Times, the Middletown Daily Mail, the Orange Country Press, the Goshen Independent, the Port Jervis Gazette, the New York Tribune, and the Times and World. In 1873, Doty assisted Joshua A. Leach in the organization of locomotive fireman of the Erie Railroad, helping found the Brotherhood of Locomotive Fireman and Enginemen by writing its constitution and by-laws. In the late 1890s, Doty worked alongside Horace W. Corey in the publication of the Middletown Sunday Forum in Middletown, New York. In 1912, Doty was elected to the New York Assembly, serving one term. Issues put before by the Assembly during Doty's term included temperance and prohibition, women's suffrage, and religious opposition to public and social activities on Sundays. In 1923, Doty helped found the Historical Society of Middletown and Wallkill Precinct, serving as president for ten years and honorary president until his death in 1936.

In his personal life, Doty was a member of several fraternal orders and social clubs, including the Port Jervis Masonic Lodge, Neversink Royal Arch Chapter, the Delaware Valley Commandery of Knights Templar, the Elks Lodge, and the Knights of Pythias.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: William T. Doty papers, #751. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.corpname: Brotherhoods of Locomotive Firemen and Enginemen

did.unittitle: Series I. Notes, Drafts, and Manuscripts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and drafts of speeches, articles and columns, letters to the editor, local history manuscripts, and press releases authored by W.T. Doty and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes handwritten draft titled "How the Present System of Taxation Oppresses the Farmer."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes historical sketch of journalism in Orange County, New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter in opposition to the running of the Middletown Forum Sunday edition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Biographical sketch of James Martin Allterton, author of "Hawk's Nest, or The Last of the Cahoonshees."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes pieces on temperance, World War I, and general American history.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains articles regarding Stephen Crane, author of "The Red Badge of Courage."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes handwritten draft of W.T. Doty's first public speech, 1863-4.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between W.T. Doty and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between W.T. Doty and the United States Department of Agriculture regarding the identification of fruits sent by mail. Includes lists of specimens sent for identification.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence regarding W.T. Doty's camapgin for and election to the New York Assembly.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence during Doty's term as New York Assemblyman. Includes correspondence regarding women's suffrage, the Dennen Sunday Baseball Bill, and correspondence and bulletins relating to then-Senator Roosevelt's Commission Merchants' Bill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence during Doty's term as New York Assemblyman. Includes correspondence regarding the Murtagh-Jackson Bill and the Foley Bill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Constituent postcards to Assemblyman Doty urging him to support or oppose certain bills.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence between members of the Brotherhood of Locomotive Fireman and Enginemen, inlcuding correspondence between founder J.A. Leach and W.T. Doty.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence related to the 150th year anniversary of the Battle of Minisink. Also includes correspondence from V.D. Doty, W.T. Doty's son.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter from the Library of Congress.

did.unittitle: Series III. Ephemera and Print Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes publications from the New York State Agricultural Experimentation Station.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a copy of the Brotherhood of Locomotive Firemen and Enginemen's Magazine, June 1939.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes posters from FDR's reelection campaign.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes alkali and soil maps for New Mexico, Utah, and Connecticut/Massachusetts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A report from the U.S. Department of Agriculture Division of Pomology regarding native and introduced spcies of nuts in the United States.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes pamphlets of Woodrow Wilson's 1912 "Democractic Principles" speech in German and Polish.

did.unittitle: Pennsylvania Second Geological Survey, Volume AC

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains full color and black and white plates of the Anthracite Coal Mine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings related to game laws.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings related to Luther R. Marsh and the American Spiritualism movement.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings related to the Middletown National Bank lawsuit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings related to fraud cases, divorce, and bigamy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of Port Jervis Gazette with front page article "B.L.F.E. Unveils Monument for W.T. Doty at Circleville."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Journals, diaries, and ledgers of W.T. Doty and his father, Jonathan F. Doty. Includes minute books for the Circleville Farm and Garden Club and the Minisink Typographical Union No 131.

did.unittitle: Minisink Typographical Union No. 131 minute book
Circleville Farm and Garden Club minute book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes newspaper clippings related to meetings and events held by the club.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains names and locations in roughly chronological order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Twelve volumes of local history newspaper clippings and notes compiled by W.T. Doty. Includes one non-local history scrapbook.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings related to the history of Port Jervis, New York, and the Battle of Minisink (1779).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings related to freemasonry and William H. Nearpass' election as Postmaster General.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings related to the Doty family and notes by V.D. Doty.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings fro the Sussex County Independent newspaper.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings related to monogamy, religion, war and conflict, socialism, and philosophy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings related to religion, philosophy, poetry, linguisitics, history, and natural disasters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings related to poetry, short stories, and anecdotal accounts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings related to W.T. Doty as a citizen and politician, his activities and views, from Port Jervis and Circleville newspapers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and clippings related to politics, socialism, and science.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings related to national and local politics (inlcuding President Taylor's inaguration), court proceedings, and labor and industry.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings of articles written by Garrett P. Serviss.
